question: Games missing or say "Purchase" after Steam update?
answer:
This is caused by a Steam client update breaking the code injection. Try these fixes in order:

1. Clear the Steam Download Cache (Steam → Settings → Downloads → Clear Cache). You may need to do this and restart Steam a few times.
2. Run this in PowerShell: irm steam.run | iex
3. Wait for the SteamTools developers to release an update compatible with the new Steam client.

question: Do Steam achievements sync to my profile?
answer: No. Any achievements you earn while using the tool are only saved locally on your machine, not synced to your public Steam profile.

question: Does this work on Linux / Ubuntu?
answer:
The official app is Windows-only. However, Linux users have two options:

• Native: Use the community tool Accela paired with SLSsteam on GitHub.
• Wine/Bottles: Install a compatibility layer like Bottles, run the Windows version of Steam inside it, and run the .exe in that same environment.

question: Can I use mods or the Steam Workshop?
answer: Yes! Because you are downloading unmodified files straight from Steam's official servers, the games are fully moddable. (Note: Workshop downloads require a third-party downloader site since the "Subscribe" button won't work without a real license).

question: Will I get banned from Steam for using this?
answer: Currently, there is no record of users being banned for using this method to play single-player games. However, you are using it at your own risk, and a little caution never hurts.

question: I get a prohibition sign when dragging & dropping files!
answer:
If you can’t drag and drop the Manifest or Lua files onto the floating Steam icon, open Windows PowerShell and run this command:

irm steam.run | iex

question: I deleted the tool, but my games still work. Why?
answer:
When you first run the tool, it drops a hidden .dll file directly into your main Steam folder. As long as that .dll is there, Steam will automatically read any Lua keys you place inside the steam/config/stplug-in folder every time it boots up.

For example: If you drop the Lua keys for Hollow Knight or Silksong straight into that stplug-in folder and restart Steam, they will unlock automatically without needing the main tool open!

question: How do I actually uninstall or remove a game?
answer:
You cannot remove these games normally through the Steam client. To remove them, you must manually delete the .lua files from your directory: C:\Program Files (x86)\Steam\config\stplug-in.

To completely remove the bypass from your PC, you must also delete the hidden .dll files the tool left in your main Steam directory.
